title: marsilea stratiotes
intro: Marsilea stratiotes, also known as water clover or five-finger fern, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the water clover family and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Marsilea stratiotes include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to improve digestion and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Marsilea stratiotes can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them as a decoction, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
